title: FARM Act
plain_summary: The FARM Act makes changes to how the government watches foreign investments in American farming. It adds the Secretary of Agriculture to a special government committee that reviews when foreign countries or companies try to buy or control American businesses. The bill requires this committee to look at any foreign investment that could take control of American farms or food companies. It also says that farming and food systems are important to national security. Every year, the government must write reports to Congress about how foreign countries are trying to influence American farming and food production.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Nutrition and Foreign Agriculture.

sections.title: Section 1. Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Foreign Adversary Risk Management Act or the FARM Act.

sections.title: Section 2. United States Agriculture Included in Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States
sections.text: This section makes changes to an existing law called the Defense Production Act of nineteen fifty. The Committee on Foreign Investment in the United States is a government group that reviews when foreign companies want to buy American businesses. This section adds the Secretary of Agriculture as a member of this committee. It also requires the committee to review any deal where a foreign company might gain control of an American farm business or agricultural company. The law now considers agriculture systems and supply chains as critical infrastructure that needs protection. Agricultural supply chains are also added to the list of critical technologies that the government watches closely. Agricultural products means items like crops, livestock, and other things produced on farms.

sections.title: Section 3. Reports on Investments by Foreign Countries in United States Agriculture Industry
sections.text: Starting one year after this law is passed and every year after that, two government officials must write reports about foreign influence in American farming. The Secretary of Agriculture and the Comptroller General (the head of the Government Accountability Office) must each study and report to Congress about foreign investments in American agriculture. Their reports must include information about foreign money coming into American farming, how foreign investment might hurt American food production and supply chains, which foreign countries pose the biggest threats to American agriculture, and how foreign governments try to steal American farming secrets, research, pricing information, or business plans.
